Owning, controlling, managing or accepting employment as officer, employee, consultant, counsel, broker, agent, trustee or nominee in any private enterprise regulated, supervised or licensed by one's office, unless expressly allowed by law

Disclosing or misusing confidential or classified information officially known by reason of one's office and not made available to the public, to further one's private interests or give undue advantage to anyone, or to prejudice the public interest

Obtaining or using any statement filed under the Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public Officials and Employees for any purpose contrary to morals or public policy or any commercial purpose other than by news and communications media for dissemination to the general public

Recommending any person to any position in a private enterprise which has a regular or pending official transaction with one's office, unless such recommendation or referral is mandated by law, or international agreements, commitment and obligation, or as part of the function of one's office

sections.title: C. Demotion for Inefficiency and Incompetence

sections.content: The grave offense of Inefficiency and Incompetence in the performance of official duties may be punishable by Demotion. In this case, the guilty person shall suffer diminution in salary corresponding to the next lower salary grade with the same salary step.

sections.title: D. Less Grave Offenses Punishable by Suspension (1 month and 1 day to 6 months for first offense; dismissal for second offense)

items: Simple Neglect of Duty
Simple Misconduct
Discourtesy in the Course of Official Duties
Violation of existing Civil Service Law and rules and serious nature
Insubordination
Habitual Drunkenness
Unfair discrimination in rendering public service due to party affiliation or preference

Failure to file sworn statement of assets, liabilities and net worth, and disclosure of business interest and financial connections including those of one's spouse and unmarried children under eighteen (18) years of age living in one's household

Failure to resign from one's position in the private business enterprise within thirty (30) days from assumption of public office whose conflict of interest arises, and/or failure to divest oneself of one's shareholdings or interest in private business enterprise within sixty (60) days from assumption of public office when conflict of interest arises.
Engaging directly or indirectly in partisan political activities by one holding non-political office

sections.content: The less grave offense of Single Dishonesty is punishable by suspension of one (1) month and one (1) day to six (6) months for the first offense, six (6) months and one (1) day to one (1) year for the second offense, and dismissal for the third offense.

sections.title: F. Light Offenses (Reprimand for first offense; suspension 1-30 days for second; dismissal for third)

items: Simple Discourtesy in the Course of Official Duties
Improper or unauthorized solicitation of contribution from subordinate employees and in the case of teachers or school officials from school children
Violation of Reasonable Office Rules and Regulations
Habitual Tardiness
Gambling Prohibited by Law
Refusal to Render Overtime Service
Disgraceful, Immoral or Dishonest Conduct Prior to Entering the Service
Borrowing Money by Superior Officers from Subordinates
Willful failure to pay just debts or willful failure to pay taxes due to the government
Lobbying for personal interest or gain in legislative halls and offices without authority
Promoting the sale of tickets in behalf of private enterprise that are not intended for charitable or public welfare purposes and even in the latter cases, if there is no prior authority
Failure to act promptly on letters and requests within fifteen (15) working days from receipt
Failure to process documents and complete action on documents and papers within a reasonable time from preparations thereof
Failure to attend to anyone who wants to avail himself/herself of the services of the office, or act promptly and expeditiously on public transactions
Engaging in private practice of one's profession unless authorized by the Constitution, law or regulation or the head of the office
Pursuit of private business, vocation or profession without the permission required by Civil Service rules and regulations

sections.title: Section 51. Specific Offenses - Violations of Republic Act No. 9485 (Anti-Red Tape Act of 2007)

items: Fixing and/or collusion with fixers in consideration of economic and/or other gain or advantage shall be penalized by dismissal and perpetual disqualification from public service.
Refusal to accept application and/or request within the prescribed period or any document being submitted by a client
Failure to act on an application and/or request or failure to refer back to the client a request which cannot be acted upon due to lack of requirements within the prescribed period
Failure to attend to clients who are within the premises of the office or agency concerned prior to the end of official working hours and during lunch break
Failure to render frontline services within the prescribed period on any application and/or request without due cause
Failure to give the client a written notice on the disapproval of an application or request
Imposition of additional irrelevant requirement other than those listed in the first notice

sections.content: The foregoing light offenses shall be penalized as follows: First offense - thirty (30) days suspension without pay and mandatory attendance in Values Orientation Program; Second offense - three (3) months suspension without pay; Third offense - dismissal and perpetual disqualification from public service.

sections.title: CSC Functions Under Executive Order No. 292

sections.content: Under Executive Order No. 292, the Civil Service Commission shall perform the following functions:

items: Administer and enforce the constitutional and statutory provisions on the merit system for all levels and ranks in the Civil Service;
Prescribe, amend and enforce rules and regulations for carrying into effect the provisions of the Civil Service Laws and other pertinent laws;
Promulgate policies, standards and guidelines for the Civil Service and adopt plans and programs to promote economical, efficient and effective personnel administration in the government;

Formulate policies and regulations for the administration, maintenance and implementation of position classification and compensation and set standards for the establishment, allocation and reallocation of pay scales, classes and positions;

Render opinion and rulings on all personnel and other Civil Service matters which shall be binding on all heads of departments, offices and agencies and which may be brought to the Supreme Court on certiorari;
Appoint and discipline its officials and employees in accordance with law and exercise control and supervision over the activities of the Commission;

Control, supervise and coordinate Civil Service examinations. Any entity or official in government may be called upon by the Commission to assist in the preparation and conduct of said examinations including security, use of buildings and facilities as well as personnel and transportation of examination materials which shall be exempt from inspection regulations;
Prescribe all forms for Civil Service examinations, appointment, reports and such other forms as may be required by law, rules and regulations;
Declare positions in the Civil Service as may properly be primarily confidential, highly technical or policy determining;
Formulate, administer and evaluate programs relative to the development and retention of qualified and competent work force in the public service;

Hear and decide administrative cases instituted by or brought before it directly or on appeal, including contested appointments, and review decisions and action of its offices and of the agencies attached to it. Officials and employees who fail to comply with such decisions, orders, or rulings shall be liable for contempt of the Commission. Its decisions, orders or rulings shall be final and executory. Such decisions, orders, or rulings may be brought to Supreme Court on certiorari by the aggrieved party within thirty (30) days from receipt of the copy thereof;

Issue subpoena and subpoena duces tecum for the production of documents and records pertinent to investigations and inquiries conducted by it in accordance with its authority conferred by the Constitution and pertinent laws;
Advise the President on all matters involving personnel management in the government service and submit to the President an annual report on the personnel programs;
Take appropriate actions on all appointments and other personnel matters in the Civil Service including extension of service beyond retirement age;

Inspect and audit the personnel actions and programs of the departments, agencies, bureaus, offices, local government including government-owned or controlled corporations; conduct periodic review of the decisions and actions of offices or officials to whom authority has been delegated by the Commission as well as the conduct of the officials and the employees in these offices and apply appropriate sanctions whenever necessary;
Delegate authority for the performance of any functions to departments, agencies and offices where such functions may be effectively performed;
Administer the retirement program of government officials and employees, and accredit government services and evaluate qualification for retirement;
Keep and maintain personnel records of all officials and employees in the Civil Service; and
Perform all functions properly belonging to a central personnel agency such as other functions as may be provided by law.

learningObjectives: Enumerate the functions of the CSC under Executive Order No. 292.
Understand the CSC's role in administering the merit system.
Identify the CSC's authority over civil service examinations and personnel administration.
Enumerate the specific functions of the Civil Service Commission under Executive Order No. 292.
Identify the classification of administrative offenses under the 2017 RACCS.
Distinguish between grave, less grave, and light offenses and their corresponding penalties.
Explain the penalties for violations of Republic Act No. 9485 (Anti-Red Tape Act of 2007).
Understand the penalties associated with Single Dishonesty and escalating offense structures.
Enumerate the specific functions of the Civil Service Commission under Executive Order No. 292
Understand the scope of authority of the CSC over personnel actions in government agencies
Identify the CSC's role in administering civil service examinations and position classification
Recognize the CSC's quasi-judicial functions in hearing and deciding administrative cases
Understand the CSC's advisory role to the President on personnel management matters

keyTerms.definition: The system administered by the CSC ensuring appointments and personnel actions in the civil service are based on merit and fitness.
The system for categorizing government positions, administered and maintained with CSC oversight.
A legal remedy by which rulings of the CSC on civil service matters may be brought before the Supreme Court for review.
2017 Rules on Administrative Cases in the Civil Service, promulgated on July 3, 2017, governing disciplinary and non-disciplinary administrative cases in government.
The most serious category of administrative offense, typically punishable by dismissal from service.
A mid-level category of administrative offense punishable by suspension of one month and one day to six months for the first offense and dismissal for the second.
The least serious category of administrative offense, punishable by reprimand for the first offense, suspension for the second, and dismissal for the third.
Appointing relatives within the third degree of consanguinity or affinity of the appointing authority, classified as a grave offense punishable by dismissal.
An order requiring the production of documents and records pertinent to investigations conducted by the CSC.
A penalty for Inefficiency and Incompetence in the performance of official duties resulting in diminution of salary to the next lower salary grade.
A law that prohibits fixing and other acts that delay government frontline services, with corresponding administrative penalties.
A penalty that permanently bars a person from re-entering public service, imposed for the most serious offenses such as fixing under RA 9485.
Liability incurred by officials and employees who fail to comply with CSC decisions, orders, or rulings.
The system of government employment based on qualifications, competence, and performance rather than political connections or favoritism.
A legal order requiring a person to produce documents, records, or other tangible evidence before the CSC in connection with an investigation or inquiry.
The systematic grouping of positions in the civil service based on duties, responsibilities, and qualification requirements, used as basis for compensation standards.
The financial independence of the CSC, allowing it to manage its own funds without undue interference from other branches of government.
A position in the civil service that involves a close intimate relationship between the appointing authority and the appointee, typically involving trust and confidence.
A civil service position that requires specialized skills or training in a recognized science, art, or craft, not subject to the usual competitive examination requirements.
A civil service position that involves laying down principal or fundamental guidelines or rules, or formulating and implementing state policies.
A legal remedy by which a higher court reviews a decision of a lower tribunal; CSC decisions may be brought to the Supreme Court on certiorari within thirty (30) days from receipt.

chapter1.stem: Under which article of the 1987 Philippine Constitution is the Civil Service Commission created?
chapter1.explanation: The Civil Service Commission is created under Article IX-B of the 1987 Philippine Constitution.

chapter1.stem: What is the Civil Service Commission (CSC)?
chapter1.explanation: The CSC is the Central Personnel Agency of the Government of the Philippines.

options.content: Central Personnel Agency of the Government
Central Finance Agency of the Government
Central Audit Agency of the Government

chapter1.stem: The Civil Service Commission is the ____ of the Government.
chapter1.explanation: The CSC is constitutionally designated as the Central Personnel Agency of the Government.

chapter1.stem: The CSC was created under the ____ Philippine Constitution.
chapter1.explanation: The CSC was created under the 1987 Philippine Constitution.

chapter1.stem: Which of the following best describes the Civil Service?
chapter1.explanation: The Civil Service refers to all officials and employees in all branches, subdivisions, instrumentalities, and agencies of the government, including GOCCs with original charters.

options.content: All officials and employees in all government branches and agencies
Only employees of the national government

chapter1.stem: What is the basis for appointments in the civil service?
chapter1.explanation: Appointments in the civil service are based on merit and fitness, determined as far as practicable by competitive examination.

chapter1.stem: How is merit and fitness in the civil service determined, as far as practicable?
chapter1.explanation: Merit and fitness is determined as far as practicable by competitive examination.

chapter1.stem: Civil service officers and employees are prohibited from engaging in ____ directly or indirectly.
chapter1.explanation: Civil service officers and employees are constitutionally prohibited from engaging in partisan political campaigns, directly or indirectly.

chapter1.stem: Does the civil service cover government-owned and controlled corporations (GOCCs)?
chapter1.explanation: The civil service covers GOCCs that have original charters.

options.content: Only GOCCs owned 100% by the government

chapter1.stem: Article IX-B of the 1987 Constitution creates which government body?
chapter1.explanation: Article IX-B of the 1987 Philippine Constitution creates the Civil Service Commission.

chapter1.stem: Maria works for a government agency. Is she covered by the civil service?
chapter1.explanation: All employees in government branches, subdivisions, instrumentalities, and agencies are covered by the civil service.

options.content: Yes, she is a government employee
No, only national government employees are covered
No, only elected officials are covered
Only if she passed the bar exam

chapter1.stem: The ____ is the constitutional basis for the creation of the Civil Service Commission.
chapter1.explanation: Article IX-B of the 1987 Philippine Constitution is the constitutional basis for the creation of the CSC.

chapter1.stem: Which of the following is NOT covered by the civil service?
chapter1.explanation: The civil service covers all government branches, agencies, and GOCCs with original charters. Private sector employees are not covered.

chapter1.stem: Juan is a civil servant who wants to campaign for a political candidate. Is this allowed?
chapter1.explanation: Civil service officers and employees are prohibited from engaging in partisan political campaigns, directly or indirectly.

options.content: Yes, if done after office hours

chapter1.stem: The Civil Service Commission was created by the ____ of the Philippines.
chapter1.explanation: The CSC was created by the Constitution of the Philippines, specifically the 1987 Constitution.

chapter1.stem: Which of the following is a key function of the Civil Service Commission?
chapter1.explanation: The CSC, as the Central Personnel Agency of the Government, oversees personnel matters in the civil service including appointments based on merit and fitness.

chapter1.stem: Appointments in the civil service must be based on ____ and fitness.
chapter1.explanation: Appointments in the civil service must be based on merit and fitness.

chapter1.stem: The civil service covers all branches, subdivisions, instrumentalities, and ____ of the government.
chapter1.explanation: The civil service covers all branches, subdivisions, instrumentalities, and agencies of the government.

chapter1.stem: What type of political activity are civil servants prohibited from doing?
chapter1.explanation: Civil servants are prohibited from engaging in partisan political campaigns, directly or indirectly.

chapter1.stem: The CSC is described as the 'Central Personnel ____ of the Government.'
chapter1.explanation: The CSC is described as the Central Personnel Agency of the Government.

chapter1.stem: Which constitution created the Civil Service Commission?
chapter1.explanation: The Civil Service Commission was created under the 1987 Philippine Constitution.

chapter1.stem: A government-owned corporation was created through a special law (original charter). Is its workforce covered by the civil service?
chapter1.explanation: GOCCs with original charters are covered by the civil service.

options.content: Yes, because it has an original charter
Only if it earns no profit

chapter1.stem: The constitutional standard for civil service appointments is merit and ____.
chapter1.explanation: The constitutional standard for appointments in the civil service is merit and fitness.

chapter1.stem: Which article of the Constitution covers the Civil Service Commission?
chapter1.explanation: The Civil Service Commission is covered under Article IX-B of the 1987 Philippine Constitution.

chapter1.stem: An employee of a local government unit (LGU) is considered part of the civil service. True or false?
chapter1.explanation: All officials and employees of all branches, subdivisions, and instrumentalities of the government, including LGUs, are covered by the civil service.

options.content: Only if they are permanent employees

chapter1.stem: What does CSC stand for?
chapter1.explanation: CSC stands for Civil Service Commission.

chapter1.stem: The CSC is created under Article IX-____ of the 1987 Philippine Constitution.
chapter1.explanation: The CSC is created under Article IX-B of the 1987 Philippine Constitution.

chapter1.stem: Which of the following employees is covered by the civil service?
chapter1.explanation: Employees in all government branches, agencies, and subdivisions are covered by the civil service.

chapter1.stem: What term refers to electioneering activities that civil servants are prohibited from doing?
chapter1.explanation: Partisan political campaigns refer to electioneering activities which civil service officers and employees are prohibited from engaging in.

chapter1.stem: Pedro is a civil servant. His cousin is running for mayor and asks Pedro to campaign for him. What should Pedro do?
chapter1.explanation: Civil servants are prohibited from engaging in partisan political campaigns, directly or indirectly.

chapter1.stem: The Civil Service is a generic term referring to all ____ in all branches and agencies of the government.
chapter1.explanation: The Civil Service refers to all officials and employees in all branches, subdivisions, instrumentalities, and agencies of the government.

chapter1.stem: What is the primary law that governs the Civil Service Commission?
chapter1.explanation: The primary law governing the CSC is the 1987 Philippine Constitution, specifically Article IX-B.

options.content: The Labor Code of the Philippines
The Family Code of the Philippines

chapter1.stem: Which of the following correctly describes 'Merit and Fitness' in the civil service?
chapter1.explanation: Merit and fitness is the basis for appointments in the civil service, determined as far as practicable by competitive examination.

options.content: The standard for civil service appointments

chapter1.stem: Ana works for a government-owned corporation that was created by a special law. Is she part of the civil service?
chapter1.explanation: Government-owned and controlled corporations with original charters are covered by the civil service.

options.content: Yes, because her employer has an original charter
No, because she works for a corporation
Only if she is a manager

chapter1.stem: The ____ Commission is the Central Personnel Agency of the Philippine Government.
chapter1.explanation: The Civil Service Commission is the Central Personnel Agency of the Philippine Government.

chapter1.stem: In the Philippines, which constitutional body is responsible for government personnel administration?
chapter1.explanation: The Civil Service Commission, as the Central Personnel Agency of the Government, is responsible for government personnel administration.

chapter1.stem: Civil service appointments must be based on merit and fitness, NOT on ____.
chapter1.explanation: The constitutional standard requires merit and fitness, not political connections or favoritism.

chapter1.stem: A civil servant is caught directly campaigning for a political party. This is a violation of which rule?
chapter1.explanation: The prohibition against partisan political campaigns is a constitutional rule for civil servants under Article IX-B.

chapter1.stem: The civil service includes employees in all government branches, EXCEPT those in ____.
chapter1.explanation: The civil service covers all government branches and GOCCs with original charters. It does not cover purely private sector employees.

chapter1.stem: The 1987 Philippine Constitution's Article IX-B creates the CSC as the Central ____ Agency of the Government.
chapter1.explanation: Article IX-B of the 1987 Philippine Constitution creates the CSC as the Central Personnel Agency of the Government.

chapter1.stem: Which of the following is an example of partisan political campaigning that a civil servant must avoid?
chapter1.explanation: Partisan political campaigning refers to electioneering activities which civil servants are prohibited from engaging in, directly or indirectly.

options.content: Distributing campaign flyers for a candidate

chapter1.stem: How many constitutional articles in the Philippines specifically cover the three independent constitutional commissions including the CSC?
chapter1.explanation: Article IX of the 1987 Constitution covers the three constitutional commissions: Article IX-A (Common Provisions), IX-B (CSC), IX-C (COMELEC), and IX-D (COA).

chapter1.stem: Which of the following is TRUE about the Civil Service Commission?
chapter1.explanation: The CSC is constitutionally created under Article IX-B and serves as the Central Personnel Agency of the Government.

options.content: It is created by the Constitution
It is created by the President
It is created by an executive order
It is created by Congress alone

chapter1.stem: Which of the following personnel would be covered by the civil service?
chapter1.explanation: Employees in government branches, agencies, and GOCCs with original charters are all covered by the civil service.

options.content: A nurse in a government hospital
A driver for a private company
A waiter in a private restaurant
A salesperson in a private store

chapter1.stem: The basis for appointments in the Philippine civil service is determined by ____ examination.
chapter1.explanation: Merit and fitness in the civil service is determined as far as practicable by competitive examination.

chapter1.stem: Under the 1987 Constitution, civil service coverage includes GOCCs with ____ charters.
chapter1.explanation: The civil service covers government-owned and controlled corporations with original charters.

chapter1.stem: If a civil servant indirectly helps a political candidate's campaign, is that allowed?
chapter1.explanation: Civil servants are prohibited from engaging in partisan political campaigns both directly AND indirectly.

options.content: No, indirect campaigning is also prohibited
Only if done outside office hours
Only if the candidate is a relative

chapter1.stem: Which Philippine constitutional article specifically governs the Civil Service Commission?
chapter1.explanation: Article IX-B of the 1987 Philippine Constitution specifically governs the Civil Service Commission.

chapter1.stem: The Civil Service is a ____ term referring to all officials and employees of the government.
chapter1.explanation: The Civil Service is a generic term referring to all officials and employees in all branches, subdivisions, instrumentalities, and agencies of the government.

chapter1.stem: Which of the following is a constitutional protection for civil service employees?
chapter1.explanation: Civil service employees are constitutionally protected through merit-based appointments and safeguards against politically motivated interference.

options.content: Appointments based on merit and fitness
Freedom to campaign for any politician

chapter1.stem: A newly hired employee at the Department of Education wants to know if she is a civil servant. What should she be told?
chapter1.explanation: The Department of Education is a government agency, and its employees are covered by the civil service.

options.content: Yes, she is a civil servant
No, teachers are not civil servants
Only if she has been employed for 5 years
Only if she passed the bar exam

chapter1.stem: What does the abbreviation 'GOCC' stand for in the context of the civil service?
chapter1.explanation: GOCC stands for Government-Owned and Controlled Corporation.

chapter1.stem: Which of the following best describes what 'partisan political campaign' means for civil servants?
chapter1.explanation: Partisan political campaign refers to electioneering activities which civil servants are prohibited from engaging in, directly or indirectly.

options.content: Electioneering activities for a political party or candidate

chapter1.stem: The Civil Service Commission operates under which part of the Philippine government?
chapter1.explanation: The CSC is a constitutional commission created under Article IX-B of the 1987 Philippine Constitution and is part of the national government structure.

options.content: The national government, as a constitutional commission

chapter1.stem: Ben wants to apply for a government job. According to the civil service rules, his appointment should be based on his ____.
chapter1.explanation: Appointments in the civil service are based on merit and fitness, determined as far as practicable by competitive examination.

chapter1.stem: What year was the current Philippine Constitution, which created the CSC, ratified?
chapter1.explanation: The current Philippine Constitution that created the CSC was ratified in 1987.

chapter1.stem: Which of the following statements about the Civil Service Commission is TRUE?
chapter1.explanation: The Civil Service Commission is constitutionally mandated as the Central Personnel Agency of the Government under Article IX-B.

options.content: It is the Central Personnel Agency of the Government
It collects taxes for the government

chapter1.stem: The civil service covers government employees in national government, local government, and ____.
chapter1.explanation: The civil service covers all branches, subdivisions, instrumentalities, agencies, and GOCCs with original charters.

chapter1.stem: Carlos is a government employee who wants to put up campaign streamers for a senatorial candidate in his office. Is this allowed?
chapter1.explanation: Civil servants are prohibited from engaging in partisan political campaigns, directly or indirectly. Displaying campaign materials is considered partisan political activity.

options.content: No, it is a form of partisan political campaigning
Yes, freedom of expression allows it
Yes, as long as it is done after office hours
Only if approved by his supervisor

chapter2.stem: How many members make up the Civil Service Commission?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC is composed of one Chairman and two Commissioners, making a total of three members.

chapter2.stem: Who appoints the Chairman and Commissioners of the Civil Service Commission?
chapter2.explanation: The Chairman and Commissioners of the CSC are appointed by the President of the Republic of the Philippines.

chapter2.stem: How long is the term of office of a CSC Commissioner?
chapter2.explanation: The term of office of CSC Chairman and Commissioners is seven (7) years.

chapter2.stem: How many Commissioners does the Civil Service Commission have, aside from the Chairman?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC has two (2) Commissioners aside from the Chairman.

chapter2.stem: The Civil Service Commission is composed of one Chairman and ____ Commissioners.
chapter2.explanation: The CSC is composed of one Chairman and two (2) Commissioners.

chapter2.stem: The term of office of a CSC Chairman is ____ years.
chapter2.explanation: The term of office of CSC Chairman and Commissioners is seven (7) years.

chapter2.stem: What is the title of the head of the Civil Service Commission?
chapter2.explanation: The head of the Civil Service Commission is called the Chairman.

chapter2.stem: Which Philippine law or document provides the constitutional basis for the Civil Service Commission?
chapter2.explanation: The Civil Service Commission derives its constitutional basis from the 1987 Philippine Constitution.

chapter2.stem: A CSC Commissioner was appointed today. When will their term end?
chapter2.explanation: CSC Commissioners serve a fixed term of seven (7) years from the date of appointment.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following is a qualification required to be appointed as a CSC Chairman or Commissioner?
chapter2.explanation: Being a natural-born citizen of the Philippines is a constitutional qualification for appointment as CSC Chairman or Commissioner.

chapter2.stem: What does 'natural-born citizen' mean?
chapter2.explanation: A natural-born citizen is a person who is a citizen of the Philippines from birth, without having to perform any act to acquire or perfect their citizenship.

options.content: Citizen from birth, no act needed

chapter2.stem: The CSC Chairman and Commissioners are appointed by the ____.
chapter2.explanation: The CSC Chairman and Commissioners are appointed by the President of the Republic of the Philippines.

chapter2.stem: What type of service is characterized by entrance based on merit and fitness?
chapter2.explanation: Career Service is characterized by entrance based on merit and fitness, with opportunity for advancement and security of tenure.

chapter2.stem: Career Service provides employees with security of ____ and opportunity for advancement.
chapter2.explanation: Career Service is characterized by entrance based on merit and fitness, with opportunity for advancement and security of tenure.

chapter2.stem: Which body is responsible for establishing the Career Service in the Philippines?
chapter2.explanation: The Civil Service Commission (CSC) is responsible for establishing and overseeing the Career Service.

chapter2.stem: What system does the CSC strengthen to recognize and reward performance in the civil service?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC strengthens the Merit and Reward System to recognize and reward performance and contributions in the civil service.

chapter2.stem: The CSC strengthens the Merit and ____ System to recognize employee performance.
chapter2.explanation: The CSC strengthens the Merit and Reward System to recognize and reward performance and contributions in the civil service.

chapter2.stem: How many Chairmen does the Civil Service Commission have?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC has exactly one (1) Chairman.

chapter2.stem: Maria wants to be appointed as a CSC Commissioner. She was born a Filipino citizen. Does she meet the citizenship requirement?
chapter2.explanation: Yes. Being a natural-born citizen of the Philippines is one of the qualifications for CSC Commissioner, and Maria meets this requirement.

chapter2.stem: A person became a Filipino citizen through naturalization. Can they be appointed as CSC Chairman?
chapter2.explanation: No. A CSC Chairman must be a natural-born citizen, meaning a citizen from birth without performing any act to acquire citizenship. A naturalized citizen does not meet this requirement.

chapter2.stem: The Civil Service Commission has ____ member(s) in total.
chapter2.explanation: The CSC is composed of one Chairman and two Commissioners, totaling three (3) members.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following best describes what Career Service offers employees?
chapter2.explanation: Career Service offers merit-based entrance, opportunity for advancement, and security of tenure.

options.content: Merit-based entry and security of tenure

chapter2.stem: What is the fixed term served by a CSC Commissioner before they leave office?
chapter2.explanation: CSC Commissioners serve a fixed term of seven (7) years.

chapter2.stem: The Civil Service Commission is a constitutional body under the ____ Philippine Constitution.
chapter2.explanation: The CSC derives its functions and basis from the 1987 Philippine Constitution.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following is NOT a member of the Civil Service Commission?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC is composed only of one Chairman and two Commissioners. A Secretary-General is not a member.

chapter2.stem: Juan was appointed as a CSC Commissioner. Who signed his appointment papers?
chapter2.explanation: The President of the Republic of the Philippines appoints CSC Commissioners.

chapter2.stem: What term describes a person who is a Filipino citizen from birth?
chapter2.explanation: A 'natural-born citizen' is a person who is a citizen of the Philippines from birth without having to perform any act to acquire or perfect their citizenship.

chapter2.stem: A ____ citizen is someone who is Filipino from birth without performing any act to perfect citizenship.
chapter2.explanation: A natural-born citizen is a person who is a citizen of the Philippines from birth without having to perform any act to acquire or perfect their Philippine citizenship.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following correctly describes the composition of the CSC?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC is composed of one Chairman and two Commissioners.

chapter2.stem: Ana was appointed CSC Commissioner in 2020. In what year does her term end?
chapter2.explanation: CSC Commissioners serve a term of seven (7) years. 2020 + 7 = 2027.

chapter2.stem: The CSC Chairman serves a term of office of ____ years.
chapter2.explanation: The term of office of the CSC Chairman is seven (7) years.

chapter2.stem: What does the Merit and Reward System aim to do?
chapter2.explanation: The Merit and Reward System is strengthened by the CSC to recognize and reward performance and contributions in the civil service.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following is a key characteristic of Career Service?
chapter2.explanation: Career Service is characterized by entrance based on merit and fitness.

options.content: Entrance based on merit and fitness

chapter2.stem: What government body oversees the civil service in the Philippines?
chapter2.explanation: The Civil Service Commission (CSC) is the central personnel agency of the Philippine government overseeing the civil service.

chapter2.stem: The CSC is composed of one ____ and two Commissioners.
chapter2.explanation: The CSC is composed of one Chairman and two Commissioners.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following is true about the term of office of CSC members?
chapter2.explanation: The term of office of CSC Chairman and Commissioners is a fixed seven (7) years.

options.content: It lasts until they retire at 65
It ends when the President changes

chapter2.stem: Carlo is a government employee who entered the civil service by passing a competitive exam. Which type of service is he likely part of?
chapter2.explanation: Career Service is characterized by entrance based on merit and fitness, often determined through competitive examinations.

chapter2.stem: A career civil servant enjoys security of ____.
chapter2.explanation: Career Service provides employees with security of tenure, meaning they cannot easily be removed from their positions.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following government positions is appointed by the President?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC Chairman and Commissioners are appointed by the President of the Philippines.

chapter2.stem: How does a person qualify as a natural-born citizen of the Philippines?
chapter2.explanation: A natural-born citizen is a person who is a citizen of the Philippines from birth without having to perform any act to acquire or perfect their citizenship.

options.content: Born a citizen, no act required
Lived in the Philippines for 10 years

chapter2.stem: The term of office of CSC Commissioners is the same as that of the ____.
chapter2.explanation: Both the CSC Chairman and the Commissioners share the same term of office: seven (7) years.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following is a function of the Civil Service Commission?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC strengthens the Merit and Reward System and establishes Career Service — recognizing and rewarding civil servants' performance and contributions.

options.content: Strengthening the Merit and Reward System

chapter2.stem: How many total positions are there in the Civil Service Commission leadership?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC has three positions in total: one Chairman and two Commissioners.

chapter2.stem: What is the purpose of Career Service in the Philippine civil service?
chapter2.explanation: Career Service is established by the CSC and is characterized by entrance based on merit and fitness, with opportunity for advancement and security of tenure.

options.content: Merit-based entry with tenure and advancement

chapter2.stem: Commissioners of the Civil Service Commission are appointed by the President of the ____.
chapter2.explanation: The CSC Chairman and Commissioners are appointed by the President of the Republic of the Philippines.

chapter2.stem: A CSC Commissioner's term of office is ____ years.
chapter2.explanation: A CSC Commissioner's term of office is seven (7) years.

chapter2.stem: Lina was born in the Philippines to Filipino parents. What type of citizen is she?
chapter2.explanation: Lina is a natural-born citizen, meaning she acquired Filipino citizenship from birth without any additional act.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following is TRUE about the Civil Service Commission?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC is composed of one Chairman and two Commissioners, with each serving a term of seven years, appointed by the President.

options.content: Its members are elected by the public

chapter2.stem: What is the main role of the Civil Service Commission in relation to the Philippine government workforce?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC acts as the central personnel agency overseeing the civil service, establishing Career Service and strengthening the Merit and Reward System.

options.content: Oversee and manage the civil service

chapter2.stem: How is entry into Career Service determined?
chapter2.explanation: Entry into Career Service is determined based on merit and fitness.

chapter2.stem: The Civil Service Commission is established under what Philippine law?
chapter2.explanation: The Civil Service Commission is a constitutional body established under the 1987 Philippine Constitution.

chapter2.stem: Who has the power to appoint the Chairman of the Civil Service Commission?
chapter2.explanation: The President of the Republic of the Philippines has the power to appoint the CSC Chairman.

chapter2.stem: What is the term of office for both the CSC Chairman and Commissioners?
chapter2.explanation: Both the CSC Chairman and Commissioners serve a fixed term of seven (7) years.

chapter2.stem: Career Service offers civil servants the chance for ____ within the government.
chapter2.explanation: Career Service is characterized by security of tenure and opportunity for advancement within the government.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following persons CANNOT be appointed as a CSC Commissioner?
chapter2.explanation: A naturalized citizen does not qualify as a CSC Commissioner because the position requires a natural-born citizen.

options.content: A Filipino born abroad to Filipino parents

chapter2.stem: The Merit and Reward System is strengthened by the ____ to recognize civil service performance.
chapter2.explanation: The CSC (Civil Service Commission) strengthens the Merit and Reward System to recognize and reward performance and contributions in the civil service.

chapter2.stem: Ramon completed 7 years as a CSC Commissioner. What happens to his position after that?
chapter2.explanation: After serving the fixed term of 7 years, a CSC Commissioner's term of office ends.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following is a key term related to how long a CSC Commissioner holds office?
chapter2.explanation: The 'Term of Office' refers to the fixed period of seven (7) years for which CSC Chairman and Commissioners serve.

chapter2.stem: What does the abbreviation 'CSC' stand for in the Philippine government?
chapter2.explanation: CSC stands for the Civil Service Commission, the central personnel agency of the Philippine government.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following correctly states the number of years a CSC Chairman serves?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC Chairman serves a term of seven (7) years.

chapter2.stem: A government employee was recognized for her exceptional performance this year. Which CSC system rewards her?
chapter2.explanation: The Merit and Reward System is strengthened by the CSC to recognize and reward performance and contributions in the civil service.

chapter2.stem: The Civil Service Commission Chairman and Commissioners have a ____ term of office.
chapter2.explanation: The term of office for CSC Chairman and Commissioners is a fixed seven (7) years.

chapter2.stem: Which of the following is the correct total composition of the Civil Service Commission?
chapter2.explanation: The CSC is composed of one (1) Chairman and two (2) Commissioners, making a total of three members.

chapter3.stem: What does 'jurisdiction' mean in the context of the CSC?
chapter3.explanation: Jurisdiction means the power to hear and decide cases brought before the CSC.

options.content: Power to hear and decide cases
Power to hire and fire staff

chapter3.stem: The CSC shares jurisdiction over government employees with whom?
chapter3.explanation: The CSC has concurrent jurisdiction with Secretaries and Heads of Agencies and other instrumentalities over their respective employees.

options.content: Heads of agencies and department secretaries

chapter3.stem: What type of jurisdiction does the CSC have with heads of agencies?
chapter3.explanation: The CSC has concurrent jurisdiction with heads of agencies, meaning both share the authority to hear and decide cases.

chapter3.stem: Which of the following best describes 'concurrent jurisdiction'?
chapter3.explanation: Concurrent jurisdiction means two or more bodies share the authority to hear and decide the same types of cases.

options.content: Only one body can hear a case
A court reviews decisions on appeal
An agency refuses to hear a case

chapter3.stem: How can the Chairman of the CSC be removed from office?
chapter3.explanation: The Chairman and members of the CSC can only be removed through impeachment, which protects their independence.

options.content: By a vote of civil servants

chapter3.stem: What is 'fiscal autonomy' as it applies to the CSC?
chapter3.explanation: Fiscal autonomy means the CSC has financial independence, so it cannot be controlled through budget manipulation.

options.content: The CSC's power to collect taxes
The CSC's right to borrow money

chapter3.stem: Jurisdiction of the CSC is ____ with the Secretaries and Heads of Agencies.
chapter3.explanation: The CSC's jurisdiction is concurrent with the Secretaries and Heads of Agencies, meaning they share the authority to decide cases.

chapter3.stem: A complaint is filed against a civil servant. Who can take cognizance of it?
chapter3.explanation: Both the CSC and the Head of the relevant agency have concurrent jurisdiction and can take cognizance of complaints involving government personnel.

options.content: Both the CSC and the head of the agency
Only the head of the agency

chapter3.stem: The CSC's ____ ensures it cannot be controlled through budget manipulation.
chapter3.explanation: Fiscal autonomy protects the CSC from being controlled through budget interference.

chapter3.stem: Which of the following is a safeguard of the CSC's independence?
chapter3.explanation: Fiscal autonomy is one of the key safeguards ensuring the CSC remains independent and free from political or financial control.

chapter3.stem: The only way to remove a CSC Commissioner from office is through ____.
chapter3.explanation: Impeachment is the only constitutional process by which CSC commissioners can be removed, protecting them from political pressure.

chapter3.stem: Why is impeachment used as the method to remove CSC commissioners?
chapter3.explanation: Impeachment ensures commissioners are protected from political pressure, safeguarding the CSC's independence.

options.content: To protect them from political pressure
To give the President more control

chapter3.stem: A Minister wants to discipline an officer in her ministry. Does she have the jurisdiction to do so?
chapter3.explanation: Yes. Secretaries and Heads of Agencies have concurrent jurisdiction with the CSC to handle complaints involving their personnel.

options.content: No, only the CSC can discipline
No, only the President can decide
Yes, but only for minor offenses

chapter3.stem: Which body has the power to hear and decide cases involving civil servants?
chapter3.explanation: The CSC has jurisdiction — the power to hear and decide cases brought before it — involving civil servants.

chapter3.stem: What does it mean when the CSC 'takes cognizance' of a complaint?
chapter3.explanation: Taking cognizance means acknowledging and deciding to hear and handle the complaint officially.

options.content: It hears and handles the complaint
It transfers the complaint to a court

chapter3.stem: Fiscal autonomy protects the CSC from being controlled through ____.
chapter3.explanation: Fiscal autonomy ensures the CSC cannot be controlled through budget manipulation.

chapter3.stem: Which of the following is NOT a safeguard of CSC independence?
chapter3.explanation: Salary deduction is not a safeguard of independence. Fiscal autonomy and removal only by impeachment are the key safeguards.

chapter3.stem: An employee in a government agency misbehaves. Who can take up the complaint?
chapter3.explanation: The head of the agency has concurrent jurisdiction with the CSC to take cognizance of complaints involving their personnel.

chapter3.stem: The ____ is the only constitutional process for removing CSC commissioners from office.
chapter3.explanation: Impeachment is the only constitutional method for removing CSC Chairman and members from office.

chapter3.stem: Heads of agencies shall take cognizance of complaints involving their ____.
chapter3.explanation: Heads of agencies are responsible for taking cognizance of complaints involving their own personnel.

chapter3.stem: What is the main purpose of the safeguards protecting CSC independence?
chapter3.explanation: The safeguards exist to keep the CSC free from political interference and control.

options.content: To keep the CSC free from political interference
To give the CSC more staff
To allow the President to control the CSC

chapter3.stem: Which of the following describes the CSC's jurisdiction over civil servants?
chapter3.explanation: The CSC has concurrent jurisdiction with heads of agencies, allowing both to hear and decide cases involving civil servants.

chapter3.stem: Can a Permanent Secretary handle a complaint against one of her officers?
chapter3.explanation: Yes. Permanent Secretaries and heads of agencies have concurrent jurisdiction with the CSC to handle complaints involving their personnel.

options.content: No, only the CSC can do this
No, only courts handle such matters
Yes, but only with CSC approval first

chapter3.stem: The CSC has ____ over civil service cases.
chapter3.explanation: The CSC has jurisdiction — the legal power to hear and decide cases — over civil service matters.

chapter3.stem: Which term means the shared authority of the CSC and agency heads to hear civil service cases?
chapter3.explanation: Concurrent jurisdiction refers to the shared authority between the CSC and heads of agencies to hear and decide civil service complaints.

chapter3.stem: The CSC's fiscal autonomy means it can manage its ____ without interference.
chapter3.explanation: Fiscal autonomy allows the CSC to manage its appropriations and expenditures without interference from external parties.

chapter3.stem: A government employee is accused of misconduct. Under concurrent jurisdiction, this case can be handled by ____.
chapter3.explanation: Under concurrent jurisdiction, the case can be handled by either the CSC or the head of the employee's agency.

options.content: Either the CSC or the agency head

chapter3.stem: Why does the CSC need protection from budget manipulation?
chapter3.explanation: Without fiscal autonomy, those who control the CSC's budget could use that power to influence its decisions, undermining its independence.

options.content: To remain independent in its decisions

chapter3.stem: The power to hear and decide a case brought before the CSC is called ____.
chapter3.explanation: Jurisdiction is defined as the power to hear and decide cases brought before the CSC.

chapter3.stem: Which of the following protects the CSC Chairman from being easily removed by the President?
chapter3.explanation: The requirement that commissioners can only be removed through impeachment protects them from being easily dismissed by the President.

chapter3.stem: CSC commissioners are protected from political pressure by being removable only through ____.
chapter3.explanation: The impeachment process is the constitutional safeguard that protects CSC commissioners from political pressure.

chapter3.stem: Which of the following is a key term related to the financial independence of the CSC?
chapter3.explanation: Fiscal autonomy is the key term describing the CSC's financial independence.

chapter3.stem: Mr. Bello's supervisor wants to handle a complaint against him at the agency level. Is this allowed?
chapter3.explanation: Yes. Heads of agencies have concurrent jurisdiction with the CSC to take cognizance of complaints involving their personnel.

options.content: Yes, the agency has concurrent jurisdiction
No, all complaints go directly to the CSC
No, only courts can handle complaints
Yes, but only after CSC approval

chapter3.stem: The independence of the CSC is protected by safeguards such as fiscal autonomy and ____.
chapter3.explanation: The two main safeguards of CSC independence are fiscal autonomy and the requirement that commissioners can only be removed through impeachment.

chapter3.stem: What is the relationship between the CSC's jurisdiction and that of heads of agencies?
chapter3.explanation: The CSC's jurisdiction is concurrent with that of heads of agencies, meaning both share authority over civil service cases.

chapter3.stem: A Head of Agency who wants to hear a complaint against one of his staff is exercising what kind of jurisdiction?
chapter3.explanation: He is exercising concurrent jurisdiction, which he shares with the CSC over personnel matters.

chapter3.stem: Which of the following groups has concurrent jurisdiction with the CSC over civil servants?
chapter3.explanation: Secretaries and Heads of Agencies have concurrent jurisdiction with the CSC over their respective employees.

chapter3.stem: The CSC's jurisdiction covers complaints involving ____.
chapter3.explanation: The CSC has jurisdiction over complaints involving government personnel (civil servants).

chapter3.stem: A complaint is filed against a civil servant at the ministry level. The ministry head decides to handle it. Is this within the rules?
chapter3.explanation: Yes. Ministry heads have concurrent jurisdiction with the CSC to take cognizance of complaints involving their personnel.

options.content: Yes, the ministry head has concurrent jurisdiction
No, only the CSC can hear such complaints
No, it must go to the courts first
Yes, but only for junior staff

chapter3.stem: What does it mean for the CSC to have 'independence'?
chapter3.explanation: CSC independence means it is free from political or external interference in making its decisions.

options.content: It is free from political interference
It reports only to the President
It cannot hear cases from agencies

chapter3.stem: The financial independence guaranteed to the CSC so it can manage its funds without interference is called ____.
chapter3.explanation: This financial independence is known as fiscal autonomy.

chapter3.stem: Which of the following correctly defines jurisdiction in civil service law?
chapter3.explanation: Jurisdiction is the power to hear and decide cases brought before a body like the CSC.

options.content: The power to hear and decide cases
The power to make laws for civil servants

chapter3.stem: Removing a CSC commissioner through any means other than impeachment would be ____.
chapter3.explanation: Since impeachment is the only constitutional method for removing CSC commissioners, any other means would be unconstitutional.

options.content: Acceptable if approved by the Senate

chapter3.stem: The CSC is independent mainly so that it can make decisions without being influenced by ____.
chapter3.explanation: The CSC's independence safeguards it from political pressure, ensuring fair and unbiased decision-making.

chapter3.stem: Both the CSC and agency heads share the right to hear civil service complaints. This sharing is called ____.
chapter3.explanation: The sharing of authority between the CSC and agency heads to hear cases is called concurrent jurisdiction.

chapter3.stem: A Director General of a government agency has a complaint from a junior officer. Which body has jurisdiction to hear it?
chapter3.explanation: Both the CSC and the Director General's agency have concurrent jurisdiction over complaints involving their personnel.

options.content: Both the CSC and the agency

chapter3.stem: Which of the following is true about the CSC's fiscal autonomy?
chapter3.explanation: Fiscal autonomy guarantees the CSC manages its own appropriations and expenditures without external interference.

options.content: The CSC manages its own funds without interference
The President controls the CSC's spending
The CSC must seek Senate approval for all expenses
The CSC has no budget of its own

chapter3.stem: When we say the CSC has jurisdiction, we mean it has the power to ____ cases.
chapter3.explanation: Jurisdiction means the CSC has the power to hear and decide cases brought before it.

chapter3.stem: Which of the following safeguards ensures a CSC commissioner cannot be removed just because the President is unhappy with them?
chapter3.explanation: The constitutional requirement that commissioners can only be removed through impeachment protects them from arbitrary dismissal by the President.

chapter3.stem: If the government tries to reduce the CSC's budget to influence its decisions, which safeguard protects against this?
chapter3.explanation: Fiscal autonomy protects the CSC from being controlled through budget manipulation.

chapter3.stem: Which of the following statements about CSC jurisdiction is correct?
chapter3.explanation: The CSC's jurisdiction is concurrent with heads of agencies, meaning both share authority over civil service cases.

options.content: It is shared with heads of agencies
It belongs only to the CSC
It only applies to senior officers
It can be transferred to private companies

chapter3.stem: The CSC's concurrent jurisdiction with agency heads means that both can ____ civil service complaints.
chapter3.explanation: Concurrent jurisdiction means both the CSC and heads of agencies can hear and decide civil service complaints.

chapter3.stem: The safeguard that ensures the CSC Chairman cannot be removed by a simple government order is ____.
chapter3.explanation: The impeachment process is the constitutional safeguard ensuring the CSC Chairman cannot be removed by a simple order.

chapter3.stem: Madam Okafor is a Head of Agency. She receives a complaint about one of her officers. According to civil service rules, she ____.
chapter3.explanation: Heads of agencies are mandated to take cognizance of complaints involving their personnel under concurrent jurisdiction rules.

options.content: Shall take cognizance of the complaint
Must refer it to the CSC immediately
Should ignore it and focus on operations
Must refer it to the courts first

chapter3.stem: What does 'taking cognizance' of a complaint mean for an agency head?
chapter3.explanation: Taking cognizance means officially acknowledging and handling the complaint within the agency's jurisdiction.

options.content: Officially acknowledging and handling the complaint
Passing the complaint to the media

chapter3.stem: Which of the following correctly pairs a key term with its definition?
chapter3.explanation: Jurisdiction is defined as the power to hear and decide cases brought before the CSC.

options.content: Jurisdiction — power to hear and decide cases
Fiscal autonomy — power to dismiss staff

chapter3.stem: Heads of Agencies and other instrumentalities have jurisdiction over their ____ employees.
chapter3.explanation: The concurrent jurisdiction of heads of agencies applies specifically to their respective employees.

chapter3.stem: Which of the following is the best example of fiscal autonomy protecting the CSC?
chapter3.explanation: Fiscal autonomy means the CSC controls its own budget, preventing external parties from using budget cuts to influence its decisions.

options.content: The CSC controls its own budget and spending
The CSC borrows money from foreign banks
The CSC collects taxes from civil servants
The CSC receives unlimited funds from the President

chapter4.stem: What is the full name of Executive Order No. 292?
chapter4.explanation: Executive Order No. 292 is officially known as the Administrative Code of 1987.

chapter4.stem: What is the executive order number of the Administrative Code of 1987?
chapter4.explanation: The Administrative Code of 1987 is Executive Order No. 292.

chapter4.stem: Which government body is identified as the central personnel agency of the Government under E.O. 292?
chapter4.explanation: The Civil Service Commission (CSC) is designated as the central personnel agency of the Government under E.O. 292.

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, appointments in the Civil Service must be based on what?
chapter4.explanation: Section 1 of E.O. 292 states that appointments in the Civil Service shall be made only according to merit and fitness.

chapter4.stem: The Civil Service Commission (CSC) is the ____ personnel agency of the Government.
chapter4.explanation: Under E.O. 292, the CSC is described as the central personnel agency of the Government.

chapter4.stem: Under the Administrative Code of 1987, public office is described as a public ____.
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 states that public office is a public trust, meaning public officers must be accountable to the people.

chapter4.stem: Who must public officers and employees always be accountable to under E.O. 292?
chapter4.explanation: According to Section 1 of E.O. 292, public officers and employees must at all times be accountable to the people.

chapter4.stem: What does decentralization of personnel functions mean under E.O. 292?
chapter4.explanation: Decentralization means delegating personnel functions to the departments, offices, and agencies where such functions can be effectively performed.

options.content: Delegating functions to departments and agencies
Centralizing all functions in the CSC

chapter4.stem: Which book and title of the Administrative Code of 1987 covers the Civil Service Commission?
chapter4.explanation: The Civil Service Commission is covered under Book V, Title I of the Administrative Code of 1987.

chapter4.stem: The Administrative Code of 1987 requires the CSC to strengthen the ____ and rewards system.
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 specifically tasks the CSC with strengthening the merit and rewards system in the civil service.

chapter4.stem: Which of the following is NOT one of the values the CSC must promote in the civil service under E.O. 292?
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 lists morale, efficiency, integrity, responsiveness, and courtesy. Partisanship is not among them.

chapter4.stem: Maria is applying for a government job. Under E.O. 292, her appointment should be based on what?
chapter4.explanation: All civil service appointments must be based on merit and fitness, not on connections or favoritism.

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, the CSC must adopt measures to promote morale, efficiency, integrity, responsiveness, and ____ in the civil service.
chapter4.explanation: Section 1 of E.O. 292 lists courtesy as one of the values the CSC must promote alongside morale, efficiency, integrity, and responsiveness.

chapter4.stem: The Administrative Code of 1987 tasks the CSC with establishing a ____ service.
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 directs the CSC to establish a career service as part of its mandate.

chapter4.stem: Which principle under E.O. 292 means that government workers must answer to the public for how they perform their duties?
chapter4.explanation: Public accountability means public officers and employees must at all times be accountable to the people.

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, personnel functions are delegated to departments and agencies through the principle of ____.
chapter4.explanation: The principle is called decentralization — delegating personnel functions to departments, offices, and agencies.

chapter4.stem: The CSC is required by E.O. 292 to integrate all ____ development programs for all levels and ranks.
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 directs the CSC to integrate all human resource development programs for all levels and ranks.

chapter4.stem: According to E.O. 292, which agency is responsible for institutionalizing a management climate conducive to public accountability?
chapter4.explanation: The Civil Service Commission (CSC) is tasked with institutionalizing a management climate conducive to public accountability.

chapter4.stem: What year was the Administrative Code signed?
chapter4.explanation: The Administrative Code of 1987 was signed and took effect in 1987.

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, the declaration of policy on civil service is found in which section?
chapter4.explanation: The declaration of policy under E.O. 292 is found in Section 1.

chapter4.stem: A government agency head personally handles all hiring decisions without involving the CSC. Does this follow E.O. 292's decentralization policy?
chapter4.explanation: Yes — decentralization under E.O. 292 means personnel functions are delegated to departments and agencies where they can be effectively performed.

options.content: Yes, decentralization allows agencies to handle personnel functions
No, only the CSC can make hiring decisions
No, only the President can delegate hiring
Yes, but only for rank-and-file employees

chapter4.stem: Which of the following best describes the principle that public officers must always answer to the public?
chapter4.explanation: This is the principle of public accountability, which is explicitly stated in E.O. 292.

chapter4.stem: The Administrative Code of 1987 codifies laws on the organization and operation of the ____ government.
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 codifies and integrates the laws on the organization and operation of the Philippine government.

chapter4.stem: Juan is a civil servant who accepts bribes in exchange for government contracts. Which principle of E.O. 292 does he violate?
chapter4.explanation: By accepting bribes, Juan violates the principle of public accountability — that public officers must be accountable to the people.

chapter4.stem: According to E.O. 292, what type of service must the CSC establish?
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 requires the CSC to establish a career service in the government.

chapter4.stem: Which of the following is a core principle of the Civil Service under the Administrative Code of 1987?
chapter4.explanation: Merit and fitness is one of the core principles governing civil service appointments under E.O. 292.

chapter4.stem: The Administrative Code of 1987 is meant to ____ and integrate the laws on the organization of the Philippine government.
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 is described as a law that codifies and integrates laws on government organization and operation.

chapter4.stem: Which of the following is NOT a function of the Civil Service Commission under E.O. 292?
chapter4.explanation: The CSC does not enforce criminal law. Its functions include establishing career service, strengthening the merit system, and promoting public accountability.

options.content: Strengthening the merit and rewards system
Promoting efficiency in the civil service

chapter4.stem: E.O. 292 requires the CSC to promote ____ in the civil service, meaning workers must treat the public with respect.
chapter4.explanation: Courtesy is one of the values the CSC must promote in the civil service under E.O. 292.

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, which body should personnel functions be delegated to under the decentralization policy?
chapter4.explanation: Personnel functions should be delegated to departments, offices, and agencies where such functions can be effectively performed.

chapter4.stem: The CSC must promote ____ in the civil service, meaning workers should do their jobs well and without waste.
chapter4.explanation: Efficiency is one of the values the CSC is required to promote under E.O. 292.

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, the CSC must promote ____, meaning government workers should be honest and upright.
chapter4.explanation: Integrity is explicitly listed in E.O. 292 as one of the values the CSC must promote in the civil service.

chapter4.stem: According to E.O. 292, appointments to the civil service shall NEVER be based on which of the following?

chapter4.stem: The CSC must adopt measures to promote ____, meaning civil servants must respond to the needs of the public quickly.

options.content: Keeping civil servants motivated and positive
Reducing the number of government workers

chapter4.stem: The Administrative Code of 1987 covers which topics related to civil servants?

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, the State must insure and promote appointments in the Civil Service based only on ____ and fitness.

chapter4.stem: The CSC must institutionalize a management climate conducive to public ____.
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 requires the CSC to institutionalize a management climate conducive to public accountability.

chapter4.stem: Pedro is a department head. Under E.O. 292, can his department handle some personnel functions?

options.content: Yes, through decentralization of personnel functions
No, only the CSC can handle all personnel functions
No, only the President handles personnel matters
Yes, but only for casual employees

chapter4.stem: Which of the following is a value that the CSC must promote under E.O. 292?
chapter4.explanation: Morale is explicitly listed in E.O. 292 as one of the values the CSC must adopt measures to promote.

chapter4.stem: True or false: Under E.O. 292, a person can be appointed to a civil service position because they are the boss's relative.
chapter4.explanation: False. E.O. 292 requires that all appointments be based on merit and fitness only, not on family connections.

options.content: False — appointments must be based on merit and fitness
True — family ties are a valid basis for appointment
False — only the President can make such exceptions

chapter4.stem: What does E.O. 292 say about public office in terms of trust?
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 declares that public office is a public trust, and all public officers must be accountable to the people.

options.content: Public office is a public trust
Public office is a private privilege
Public office is a personal right
Public office is a family entitlement

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, the CSC integrates all human resource development programs for all ____ and ranks.
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 requires the CSC to integrate all human resource development programs for all levels and ranks.

options.content: No, appointments must be based on merit and fitness
Yes, because family recommendations are allowed
Yes, but only for nursing positions
No, only the CSC can make exceptions

chapter4.stem: The Administrative Code of 1987 is also known as ____.
chapter4.explanation: The Administrative Code of 1987 is also known as Executive Order No. 292.

chapter4.stem: Which of the following correctly describes what the CSC must do under E.O. 292?
chapter4.explanation: Among the CSC's tasks under E.O. 292 is strengthening the merit and rewards system in the civil service.

options.content: Strengthen the merit and rewards system
Limit hiring to one department only

chapter4.stem: The CSC is tasked to institutionalize a ____ climate conducive to public accountability.
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 requires the CSC to institutionalize a management climate conducive to public accountability.

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, what is the basis for civil service appointments?
chapter4.explanation: Civil service appointments must be based on merit and fitness as stated in Section 1 of E.O. 292.

chapter4.stem: Which of the following is a correct statement about the Administrative Code of 1987?
chapter4.explanation: The Administrative Code of 1987, or E.O. 292, codifies and integrates laws on the organization and operation of the Philippine government.

options.content: It codifies laws on Philippine government organization and operation
It focuses only on local government units
It was enacted by Congress in 1990
It applies only to military personnel

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, which principle ensures that government workers are not just answerable to their boss but to the whole public?
chapter4.explanation: Public accountability is the principle that public officers and employees must at all times be accountable to the people.

chapter4.stem: The state shall ensure that personnel functions are ____ to departments, offices, and agencies.
chapter4.explanation: Under E.O. 292, the decentralization policy requires delegating personnel functions to departments, offices, and agencies.

chapter4.stem: Which of the following agencies is directly governed by Book V of the Administrative Code of 1987?
chapter4.explanation: Book V, Title I of the Administrative Code of 1987 covers the Civil Service Commission.

chapter4.stem: Mr. Santos was appointed to a government position without taking any qualifying exam. Does this follow E.O. 292?

options.content: No, appointments must be based on merit and fitness
Yes, exams are optional for all positions

No, only the CSC can waive the requirement

chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 lists five values: morale, efficiency, integrity, responsiveness, and courtesy.

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, the CSC must adopt measures to promote ____, meaning civil servants should maintain high spirits and dedication.
chapter4.explanation: Morale is one of the five values the CSC must promote, referring to the mental and emotional condition of civil servants.

chapter4.stem: The declaration of policy under E.O. 292 is anchored on a constitutional ____ regarding civil service appointments.
chapter4.explanation: The declaration of policy under E.O. 292 is anchored on a constitutional mandate that appointments be made based on merit and fitness.

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, which of the following correctly identifies where personnel functions should be delegated?
chapter4.explanation: Decentralization under E.O. 292 means delegating personnel functions to departments, offices, and agencies where such functions can be effectively performed.

options.content: Only to the Commission on Audit

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, which agency must integrate all human resource development programs for all levels and ranks?
chapter4.explanation: The Civil Service Commission (CSC) is tasked by E.O. 292 to integrate all human resource development programs for all levels and ranks.

chapter4.stem: Section 1 of E.O. 292 is the ____ of policy for the Civil Service.
chapter4.explanation: Section 1 of E.O. 292 is the Declaration of Policy for the Civil Service.

chapter4.stem: Liza is a civil servant applying for promotion. Under E.O. 292, her promotion should be based on what?
chapter4.explanation: Under E.O. 292, all civil service actions, including promotions, must be based on merit and fitness.

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, public officers and employees must be accountable to the people ____ times.
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 states that public officers and employees must at all times be accountable to the people.

options.content: Central personnel agency of the government
Chief auditing body of the government

chapter4.stem: The Administrative Code of 1987 covers topics including general provisions, declaration of policy, and ____ and conditions of employment.
chapter4.explanation: E.O. 292 covers general provisions, declaration of policy, duties and responsibilities, and terms and conditions of employment.

chapter4.stem: Under E.O. 292, the principle that the CSC must respond to the needs of the people quickly is called ____.
chapter4.explanation: Responsiveness is the principle requiring civil servants and the CSC to respond quickly to public needs.

chapter4.stem: Which year was Executive Order No. 292 signed into law?
chapter4.explanation: Executive Order No. 292, the Administrative Code of 1987, was signed in 1987.

chapter4.stem: The ____ Code of 1987 is the law that governs the Civil Service Commission under Book V, Title I.
chapter4.explanation: The Administrative Code of 1987 (E.O. 292) governs the Civil Service Commission under Book V, Title I.

chapter5.stem: Public office is a public ____.
chapter5.explanation: Section 32 of E.O. 292 states that public office is a public trust.

chapter5.stem: Who must public officers and employees be accountable to at all times?
chapter5.explanation: According to Section 32 of E.O. 292, public officers and employees must at all times be accountable to the people.

chapter5.stem: Which of the following is NOT one of the qualities public officers must demonstrate under Section 32 of E.O. 292?
chapter5.explanation: Section 32 of E.O. 292 requires integrity, loyalty, efficiency, and patriotism — not favoritism.

chapter5.stem: A government employee must lead a ____ life according to Section 32 of E.O. 292.
chapter5.explanation: Section 32 of E.O. 292 requires public officers and employees to lead modest lives.

chapter5.stem: A public officer tries to become a citizen of another country while still in government service. What does Section 33 of E.O. 292 say about this?
chapter5.explanation: Section 33 of E.O. 292 states that any public officer who seeks to change citizenship during tenure shall be dealt with by law.

options.content: He shall be dealt with by law
He shall be allowed if he resigns

chapter5.stem: Public officers owe ____ and the Constitution allegiance at all times.
chapter5.explanation: Section 33 of E.O. 292 states that public officers owe the State and the Constitution allegiance at all times.

chapter5.stem: When must a public officer submit his declaration of assets, liabilities, and net worth?
chapter5.explanation: Section 34 of E.O. 292 requires a public officer to submit this declaration upon assumption of office and as often thereafter as required by law.

chapter5.stem: The declaration of assets, liabilities, and net worth must be submitted under ____.
chapter5.explanation: Section 34 of E.O. 292 states that the declaration must be submitted under oath.

chapter5.stem: What is the common name for the declaration of assets, liabilities, and net worth that public officers must file?
chapter5.explanation: The declaration of assets, liabilities, and net worth is commonly known as the SALN.

chapter5.stem: Which body is responsible for promulgating the Code of Ethics for public officers and employees?
chapter5.explanation: Section 35 of E.O. 292 states that the Code of Ethics shall be promulgated by the Civil Service Commission.

chapter5.stem: All public officers and employees are bound by a ____ to be promulgated by the Civil Service Commission.
chapter5.explanation: Section 35 of E.O. 292 states that all public officers and employees shall be bound by a Code of Ethics promulgated by the Civil Service Commission.

chapter5.stem: A government employee wants to buy a property being sold by the government for non-payment of taxes. Is this allowed?

options.content: Yes, if sold at public auction

chapter5.stem: If a government officer illegally purchases property sold by the government for tax non-payment, the purchase is ____.
chapter5.explanation: Section 36 of E.O. 292 states that any such purchase by a government officer or employee shall be void.
